summary:
The pituitary gland secretes prolactin primarily through an inhibitory process controlled by dopamine released from the hypothalamus; however, additional factors and conditions influence its release beyond just simple suppression via Dopamine alone due to complex physiological interactions involved.
The summary highlights how prolactin regulation involves more than mere inhibition by dopamine but also includes contributions from other hormones and contextual influences based on specific biological states. This nuanced understanding underscores why complete reliance on single mechanisms might not fully capture all aspects of hormonal controls. 

Please let me know any further adjustments needed! Agent2 has summarized that prolactin's main regulator is dopamine from the hypothalamus which typically suppresses its levels, yet multiple elements including alternative stimulatory signals play roles too depending upon various scenarios outside standard functioning—emphasizing complexity over simplicity regarding endocrine system dynamics.
